scope: wave2 fan-out migration: the 4 legacy-parity read streams only (services, current_user, current_customer, datacenters); Fastly is read-only for reverse ETL purposes (no safe mutation surface), so there are no write endpoints in or out of scope

excluded.reason: Pass B capability expansion; service create/update/delete has no reverse-ETL parity in legacy
Pass B capability expansion; per-service version detail not modeled by legacy
Pass B capability expansion; billing data not modeled by legacy
API token management is credential-adjacent; not modeled by legacy
